scope: AVA desktop frontend vs Goose, OpenCode, Continue — sidebar / input / status-bar / activity-bar

sidebar_gaps.issue: Session items have no message-count or token-count displayed inline. Goose shows message count + total_tokens on every card; Continue shows message count alongside timestamps. AVA shows message count only in the sub-line but omits tokens entirely.
sidebar_gaps.fix: Add a compact stat row at the bottom of each SessionItem: message count (MessageSquare icon, already imported) + token count (Cpu icon) pulled from SessionWithStats. Keep it below the date line, text-[10px] text-[var(--gray-7)].

sidebar_gaps.issue: Hover actions (Rename/Delete) are currently only two buttons with opacity-0/group-hover:opacity-100. Goose exposes five actions (rename, duplicate, delete, export, open-in-new-window) on hover. AVA's right-click context menu has more options than the hover state, creating an inconsistent mental model.
sidebar_gaps.fix: Add a Duplicate button (Copy icon) to the hover action strip, matching the context menu's duplicate option. This surfaces the most useful secondary action at hover time without overflow. Keep it to three buttons maximum (Rename, Duplicate, Delete).

sidebar_gaps.issue: Date group labels (Today / Yesterday / This Week etc.) use uppercase tracking-wider font-mono styling with text-[11px]. Goose uses plain `text-text-secondary` h2 headings without uppercase transformation. AVA's choice is defensible but the ALL CAPS + font-mono combination competes visually with session titles. The label needs to recede more.
sidebar_gaps.fix: Drop `uppercase` and `font-mono` from the group label. Use text-[10px] font-medium tracking-wide text-[var(--gray-6)] — same semantic weight but less assertive. This matches Continue's approach of neutral section separators.

sidebar_gaps.issue: The 'New chat' button is a purple square 26x26 with a Plus icon inside the SessionsToolbar. Goose uses a full-width 'New Gooseling' button at the top of the sidebar (spans the entire sidebar width, prominent). Continue's sidebar shows a '+' in the header but with a label. AVA's button is too small for its importance — new session is the primary CTA.
sidebar_gaps.fix: Replace the 26x26 icon-only button with a wider pill button that includes a text label 'New chat' visible when sidebar width > 180px, collapsing to icon-only below that threshold. Apply `bg-[var(--accent)] text-white` to give it primary-action weight.

sidebar_gaps.issue: The ProjectDropdown is in the sidebar toolbar between the Sessions title and the view-toggle. Its max-width of 120px truncates most project names at typical sidebar widths (220–260px). The chevron-down affordance is present but the button label 'No project' when no project is set is not actionable.
sidebar_gaps.fix: Extend the max-width to `calc(var(--sidebar-width) - 100px)` so it uses available space. Change fallback label to 'Open project...' (with italic styling) so it doubles as a CTA to open a project.

sidebar_gaps.issue: Active session uses `bg-[var(--gray-3)]` which is a flat background. Goose uses `hover:shadow-default` with card elevation changes. AVA's active state could use a left accent bar (like VS Code) for stronger visual cue rather than just background color, which is easy to miss in dark themes.
sidebar_gaps.fix: Add a 2px left border accent bar on the active session row: `border-l-2 border-[var(--accent)] pl-[calc(0.75rem - 2px)]` when isActive. Also shift the background to `var(--accent-subtle)` (slightly tinted) instead of neutral `var(--gray-3)`.

sidebar_gaps.issue: No skeleton loading state for the session list itself — only the overall SidebarSessionsSkeleton is exported. Within SidebarSessions, sessions() returns an empty array synchronously before load completes, which shows the 'No chats yet' empty state briefly on every open.
sidebar_gaps.fix: Add an isLoading signal from the session store to SidebarSessions and render the existing SidebarSessionsSkeleton within the list area while loading, instead of falling through to the empty state. Goose uses this pattern (showSkeleton + showContent transition) to prevent flicker.

input_gaps.issue: The textarea has `pr-[110px]` hardcoded to make room for the submit button, but the submit button group (Cancel/Send) has no precise horizontal anchor. On narrow windows this right-padding clips if the button overflows. Goose uses absolute positioning with explicit `right-2 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2` for their send button cluster.
input_gaps.fix: Keep absolute positioning for the button cluster but set `right-3` and constrain the textarea padding to `pr-[4.5rem]` with a min-width guard. Add a padding sentinel approach: measure the button width and apply it as a CSS variable `--composer-btn-width`.

input_gaps.issue: The textarea border uses `border-[var(--gray-5)]` at rest and `focus:border-[var(--accent)]` — standard and correct. However, the background is `bg-[var(--gray-3)]` (same as sidebar items) rather than a surface color. This makes the input merge with surrounding panels visually. Goose uses `bg-input` (a dedicated token) for their textarea; Claude.ai uses a slightly elevated surface.
input_gaps.fix: Change textarea background to `bg-[var(--surface-input, var(--gray-2))]`, introducing a dedicated input background CSS variable in the theme system, or use `var(--gray-2)` which is slightly lighter/darker than `var(--gray-3)` depending on theme.

input_gaps.issue: The toolbar strip below the textarea packs too many items at small widths. At sidebar-open + narrow window scenarios, the strip's `overflow-x-auto` causes horizontal scroll without any visual affordance. Goose wraps the bottom bar into two rows on narrow widths. Continue keeps only the mode indicator and model in the bar — everything else is hidden.
input_gaps.fix: Wrap the toolbar strip items into priority groups. At widths < 560px, hide the PlanActSlider label text (keep icon only), hide the DelegationToggle label, collapse the ReasoningDropdown to icon-only. Use CSS `@container` queries or a width signal.

input_gaps.issue: The model selector pill `bg-[var(--gray-3)] border-[var(--gray-5)]` renders in a flat pill with a chevron-down BEFORE the model name (chevron leading). Standard convention (Goose, OpenCode, Claude.ai) places chevron after the text. Leading chevron suggests a collapsed state rather than a dropdown trigger.
input_gaps.fix: Move the ChevronDown icon to after the model name text: `{props.currentModelDisplay()}`. This matches the universal dropdown affordance pattern.

input_gaps.issue: The attachment bar (attachment-bar.tsx) and the toolbar strip are separate rows. Goose integrates file attach, voice, and mode all in a single bottom bar. AVA has the attachment-bar above the toolbar-strip, creating two visually distinct control rows below the textarea that increase total input area height.
input_gaps.fix: Merge the attachment trigger button (paperclip) into the toolbar-strip left group alongside the model selector. Only expand into a separate attachment chip row when files are actually pending. This reduces idle input height and matches the Goose / OpenCode single-bar pattern.

input_gaps.issue: Placeholder text is static and does not adapt based on context (mode, whether agent is running, whether there are queued messages). Goose's ChatInput placeholder changes based on YOLO mode, recipe mode, etc. Continue uses 'Ask anything, @ for context, / for slash commands, ! for shell mode' — actively teaching the interface affordances.
input_gaps.fix: Make placeholder dynamic in use-input-state.ts: idle default = 'Message AVA... (/ for commands, @ for files)', plan-mode = 'Plan a task... (read-only exploration)', agent-running = 'Steer the agent... (Enter to inject)', queued messages present = 'Queued: N message(s) — /queue to view'.

status_bar_gaps.issue: The status bar is right-aligned inside the toolbar strip and shows up to 8+ distinct items simultaneously (elapsed, turn, tool name, queued count, token count, stash, warning icon, compact button, progress bar, percentage, diagnostics, cost, streaming estimate, message count). This is cognitively overwhelming and the 10px font makes it extremely small. Continue's BottomStatusBar shows maximum 4 items at a time with clear visual separation.
status_bar_gaps.fix: Implement a priority-based visibility system: during streaming show only [elapsed + turn + tool] ; at idle show [token% bar + cost] ; show message count only via tooltip on the token bar. Move diagnostics (error/warning counts) to a dedicated indicator dot on the ActivityBar or a floating badge. Limit simultaneous visible items to 5.

status_bar_gaps.issue: The token/context progress bar is only 16px wide (w-16 / 64px) and hidden behind the showTokens toggle. Continue's ContextPercentageDisplay is always visible as a prominent percentage indicator with color changes. Goose shows token count as part of the working-dir bar. AVA's bar being opt-in and tiny means most users never see context pressure building until the warning triangle appears.
status_bar_gaps.fix: Make the context bar always visible (remove the showTokens toggle, or default it to on). Expand to w-24 (96px). Add the percentage label beside it always (not behind toggle). Reserve the warning/compact button as the escalation signal at 80%/threshold.

status_bar_gaps.issue: The 'Compact' text button at context threshold is styled text-[10px] text-[var(--warning)], blending into the status bar noise. Continue uses a dedicated CompactButton component with a visible border. The low contrast at 10px makes it easy to miss when context is actually critical.
status_bar_gaps.fix: Style the compact trigger as a pill button: `px-2 py-0.5 text-[10px] border border-[var(--warning)] rounded-full bg-[var(--warning-subtle)] text-[var(--warning)]`. Add a pulse animation when the context bar is > 90% to signal urgency.

status_bar_gaps.issue: Session cost is shown in the status bar (`formatCost(sessionTokenStats().totalCost)`) but only when > 0. There is no per-request cost indicator after each agent turn. Goose's CostTracker shows running total + per-session breakdown in the bottom bar. Continue shows cost in verbose mode only.
status_bar_gaps.fix: Keep session cost in the status bar but add a tooltip that breaks down cost per turn (last turn cost + session total). Show it as '$X.XXXX' format with green color only when total > $0.001, hide below that threshold to reduce noise in cheap sessions.

status_bar_gaps.issue: The active tool label is truncated at max-w-[120px] and shows the raw tool name + summarized args. For long tool calls (e.g., 'editing src/components/long/path/to/file.tsx') this truncation loses the most informative part. Goose shows working directory in the status bar as an anchor. Continue shows `ActionStatus` as a separate widget above the input.
status_bar_gaps.fix: Expand active-tool truncation to max-w-[200px]. Add a title tooltip with the full path/args. When the tool name is 'edit' or 'write', prefer to show just the filename (basename) rather than the full summarized args.

activity_bar_gaps.issue: The activity bar has only two activity items (Sessions, Explorer) plus Settings and Toggle. VS Code and Cursor also have extensions, search, source control in the bar. While AVA intentionally keeps it minimal, the bar has significant empty vertical space between the two activity icons and the bottom controls, making it feel underused / empty.
activity_bar_gaps.fix: Add a third activity: 'Tools' or 'MCP' (Plug icon) that opens a panel showing active MCP servers and loaded tools. This fills the visual gap and gives the MCP/tool surface discoverability without cluttering the UI. Alternatively add a Memory activity (Brain icon) to jump to the memory store.

activity_bar_gaps.issue: The active indicator is a left-side 3px accent line (`absolute left-0 top-1.5 bottom-1.5 w-[3px] rounded-r-full`). This is correct VS Code pattern. However it renders behind the icon button container, and the button background `bg-[var(--alpha-white-8)]` on active creates a large 32x32 filled square. The combination is visually noisy — both the side bar AND the square highlight fire simultaneously.
activity_bar_gaps.fix: Choose one affordance: either the left-side accent bar (cleaner, matches VS Code exactly) OR the background square. Drop the `bg-[var(--alpha-white-8)]` background from active state, keep only the left bar. The bar alone is sufficient to communicate active state.

activity_bar_gaps.issue: The Settings button at the bottom has no visual distinction from the sidebar-toggle button — both are the same size, same muted color, same hover treatment, only separated by `gap-0.5`. The Settings button should carry more visual weight as a persistent destination. Goose uses a full gear icon with slightly larger touch target (40x40) and a distinct section separator.
activity_bar_gaps.fix: Add a `border-t border-[var(--border-subtle)] pt-2 mt-1` separator between the toggle button and settings button. Give settings button a slightly different hover background `hover:bg-[var(--surface-raised)]` to distinguish it as a navigation destination vs the toggle which is a state switch.

activity_bar_gaps.issue: Tooltips use the native `title` attribute (browser tooltip). These appear with a 500ms+ delay, render in the system font at OS-default position, and have no custom styling. Goose uses a custom Tooltip component (TooltipProvider/TooltipTrigger/TooltipContent) with consistent positioning (`side='right'`), theme-consistent backgrounds, and instant show after 200ms.
activity_bar_gaps.fix: Replace `title` attributes on all ActivityBar buttons with the project's existing custom Tooltip component (wrapped in TooltipProvider). Set `placement='right'` and `delay={200}` for immediate feedback. This applies to Sessions, Explorer, Toggle, and Settings buttons.

activity_bar_gaps.issue: The logo/branding in the activity bar is a 24x24 `bg-[var(--accent)]` square with a Sparkles icon. The Sparkles icon has no semantic differentiation from the 'thinking' mode in other parts of the app (where Sparkles is reused). The logo should be distinct and brand-anchored.
activity_bar_gaps.fix: Replace the Sparkles icon inside the logo mark with an SVG of AVA's actual mark (or a custom geometric icon). If no brand mark exists yet, use a triangle/pyramid shape to distinguish from the functional Sparkles icon elsewhere in the UI. The 24x24 size is appropriate; keep the `bg-[var(--accent)]` background.

priority_fixes: 1. STATUS BAR: Reduce simultaneous visible items to ≤5 with a priority visibility system — during streaming show [elapsed + turn + tool], at idle show [token-bar + cost]. The current 8–14 simultaneous data points are cognitively overwhelming at 10px font. File: src/components/chat/message-input/status-bar.tsx

2. INPUT TOOLBAR: Move ChevronDown to after model name in ModelSelector (leading chevron is wrong affordance pattern). Simultaneously merge the attachment trigger into the toolbar strip left group so the input area has one control row instead of two when no files are attached. Files: model-selector.tsx, attachment-bar.tsx, toolbar-strip.tsx

3. SIDEBAR: Strengthen active session state with a left-side 2px accent bar + `bg-[var(--accent-subtle)]` background, replacing the barely-visible flat `bg-[var(--gray-3)]`. Also add Duplicate to hover actions. Files: SessionItem.tsx

4. ACTIVITY BAR: Replace browser `title` tooltip attributes with the custom Tooltip component (side='right', 200ms delay) on all four buttons. The native title delay and system-font rendering is substandard. File: ActivityBar.tsx

5. SIDEBAR: Enlarge the 'New chat' button from a 26x26 icon to a wider pill with text label visible at full sidebar width, icon-only when collapsed. This is the primary CTA in the sidebar and deserves primary-button visual weight. File: SessionsToolbar.tsx
